steps.vulnerability_name: Harden ValidateCode — accept a plain string and build the MongoDB query internally
steps.description: The original signature accepted a raw bson.M from the caller, allowing the caller to pass attacker-controlled MongoDB operators directly into the query. Changing the parameter to a plain string and constructing bson.M{"coupon_code": couponCode} inside the model ensures only a string equality filter is ever used.

steps.vulnerability_name: Rewrite ValidateCoupon — unmarshal into a typed struct and pass only the coupon_code string
steps.description: The controller was unmarshalling the request body into a bson.M and forwarding it verbatim to the database query, enabling NoSQL operator injection. Replacing it with a models.Coupon struct ensures only the string coupon_code field is extracted before being passed to ValidateCode.

steps.vulnerability_name: Update coupon_controller imports — remove bson, add errors
steps.description: After rewriting ValidateCoupon, the bson package is no longer imported by the controller. The errors package is added for the new empty-code guard.

steps.vulnerability_name: Create NoOperatorBody middleware — defense-in-depth rejection of MongoDB operator keys
steps.description: New middleware that reads the request body, restores it for downstream handlers, and returns 400 if the body contains any key starting with '$'. This blocks operator-injection payloads before they reach controller logic.

steps.vulnerability_name: Wire NoOperatorBody middleware onto the validate-coupon route
steps.description: Add the NoOperatorBody middleware to the validate-coupon route so operator-injection payloads are rejected before reaching the controller.

verification.description: Operator injection payload must be rejected with 400 — sending {"coupon_code": {"$gt": ""}} must not return a coupon
verification.curl: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8888/identity/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"user@example.com","password":"Passw0rd"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token',''))"); curl -s -w '\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8888/community/api/v2/coupon/validate-coupon -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"coupon_code":{"$gt":""}}'

verification.description: Empty coupon_code must be rejected with 400
verification.curl: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8888/identity/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"user@example.com","password":"Passw0rd"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token',''))"); curl -s -w '\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8888/community/api/v2/coupon/validate-coupon -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"coupon_code":""}'

verification.description: Valid coupon code must still return 200
verification.curl: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8888/identity/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"user@example.com","password":"Passw0rd"}' | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin).get('token',''))"); curl -s -w '\nHTTP_STATUS:%{http_code}' -X POST http://localhost:8888/community/api/v2/coupon/validate-coupon -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -d '{"coupon_code":"TRAC075"}'
